candidates.text:
To write a limerick with rhyme and wit  
I must make sure the meter fits  
The first and second lines rhyme with the third  
And fourth and fifth lines must be heard  
Then the poem is done and it’s a hit

The task of writing is a tricky thing  
To craft a poem that makes you sing  
A line must rhyme  
A meter climb  
And limerick magic start to spring

A writer wants a limerick quick
With rhymes that are fast and slick
But the lines are too long
And the meter is wrong
So he stops right there and picks a stick

To write a limerick is quite the task
It often feels like a heavy mask
I try to rhyme with speed
But end up in need
Of help with this rhyming task
